consistent_facts.item: Top-level interface has 13 ports: clk, rst_n, valid_i, ready_o, dividend_i[64], divisor_i[64], signed_op_i, valid_o, ready_i, quotient_o[64], remainder_o[64], div_by_zero_o, overflow_o, busy_o. All use _i/_o convention consistently.
Handshake protocol: valid/ready pair on both input and output sides. No pipelining (busy=1 → ready=0).
WIDTH=64 defines all primary datapath widths. PR_WIDTH=68 for CSA redundant representation. Q_WIDTH=64 for OTF accumulators. MAX_ITER=32 for K_max=62.
All parameter derivation chains are consistent: MAX_ITER=(K_max+3)>>1 with K_max=62 (bounded by |D|=1 short-circuit). Q_WIDTH=2*MAX_ITER=64.
Single clock domain: clk rising edge. Single reset: rst_n async active low. No CDC required.

Two exception conditions: D=0 (div_by_zero) and INT64_MIN/-1 (overflow). Priority-ordered: div_by_zero > overflow. Both are non-recoverable within the module — software must handle after output handshake.
Five short-circuit paths (priority order): D=0, INT64_MIN/-1, A=0, |A|<|D|, |D|=1. All bypass ITERATE/CORRECT entirely.
QDS parameters: QDS_PR_BITS=7, QDS_DIV_BITS=3. M1/M2 threshold table with ρ=2/3 covers all 8 d_hat values × 128 pr_est values.

risky_assumptions.description: 2.5 GHz target (400 ps): First synthesis with ASAP7 RVT xp33 cells showed CORRECT CPA WNS = -6836 ps at 2 GHz (500 ps). Even after CSA optimization (iteration loop <500 ps), the 68-bit CPA in CORRECT stage needs re-evaluation at 2.5 GHz. Likely requires SLVT cells, larger drive strengths, or further CPA pipelining.
risky_assumptions.mitigation: Schedule a targeted re-synthesis at 2.5 GHz with ASAP7 SLVT cells. If CPA remains non-convergent, split CORRECT CPA into 2+ pipeline stages (currently already 3: CORRECT+CORRECT2+OUTPUT, may need more).

risky_assumptions.description: Design name 'div_srt' conflicts with all existing RTL module names ('div_srt4_*' prefix), testbench references ('Vdiv_srt4_top'), and design doc terminology. This will cause integration issues when connecting to the CPU core unless resolved.
risky_assumptions.mitigation: Plan a systematic rename pass in Phase 3: div_srt4_* → div_srt_* across all RTL, TB, sim_main.cpp, Makefile, and design doc. The rename is mechanical but must be complete (12+ files affected).

risky_assumptions.description: SRT-8 upgrade path: Current architecture (PR_WIDTH=68, Q_WIDTH=64, MAX_ITER=32, QDS table with ρ=2/3) is SRT-4 specific. SRT-8 will require: wider PR (fractionally), larger Q (N_max≈21 → Q_WIDTH=42? or different), entirely different QDS table (7-bit × ? = much larger), and possibly different ρ. The two may not share a datapath.
risky_assumptions.mitigation: Decide in Phase 2 architecture planning: (a) separate SRT-4 and SRT-8 modules, (b) unified datapath with mode select, or (c) SRT-8 as a separate future project.

risky_assumptions.description: Non-pipelined design: busy=1 blocks new operations. For a CPU execution unit, this means the divider stalls the pipeline until completion (max 37 cycles at 2.5 GHz = 14.8 ns). Some CPUs use pipelined dividers or replicated units to hide latency.
risky_assumptions.mitigation: Confirm with CPU integration team whether non-pipelined behavior is acceptable. If higher throughput is needed, consider: (a) full pipelining (back-to-back), (b) dual module replication, or (c) early-out with result forwarding.

software_visible_behavior_check.text: The module has no memory-mapped configuration registers. All control is through the valid/ready handshake. This is consistent with an execution unit design and requires no register access layer.
software_visible_behavior_check.resolution: Accept as-designed. No register extraction needed.

software_visible_behavior_check.text: Two exception flags (div_by_zero_o, overflow_o) are output with results. Software must sample them on the same cycle as valid_o=1. No interrupt path exists — software must poll or use the handshake completion.
software_visible_behavior_check.resolution: Accept as-designed. No interrupt controller integration needed.

software_visible_behavior_check.text: Software sees busy_o=1 while division is in progress. No out-of-order completion or early termination mechanism exists.
software_visible_behavior_check.resolution: Accept as-designed. Standard blocking execution unit.

recommended_clarifications.question: Output signal behavior during IDLE: Are quotient_o/remainder_o held at last value, zeroed, or undefined when valid_o=0?
recommended_clarifications.default_assumption: Held at last result (registered outputs). Downstream logic should only sample on valid_o=1.

recommended_clarifications.question: 2.5 GHz frequency target: Is this a hard requirement (must close timing at 2.5 GHz) or a target with margin (and 2 GHz is acceptable as fallback)?
recommended_clarifications.default_assumption: 2.5 GHz is the design target; if unachievable with reasonable PPA, 2 GHz is acceptable.

recommended_clarifications.question: SRT-8 timeline: Is SRT-8 in scope for the current project, or is it a separate follow-up? This affects whether the architecture should be designed for extensibility now.
recommended_clarifications.default_assumption: SRT-8 is a separate phase after SRT-4 completion and tape-out.
